title: Investigate + propose optimizations to curmudgeon's pre-review dispatcher (mode selection, queue/notes/tracker evaluation) — reduce time-to-review without compromising review quality
summary: PROP-020 successfully cut Priority 3 change-detection cost (now ~95% reduction via integrity-precomputed candidate list). But during the manual curmudgeon run tonight, it was visibly slow getting from session-start to actually-doing-the-review — the dispatcher phase (Priority 1 queue check, Priority 2 human notes scan, mode selection logic) appears to carry meaningful overhead independent of Priority 3. Operator wants tinker to investigate the pre-review pipeline holistically and find optimizations that reduce time-to-review. Constraint: do NOT compromise the actual review work (Lens A/B/C, advocate mode, cross-WIN consistency, quantitative verification). Optimize ONLY the path to review, not the review itself. PROPOSE ONLY.

context.trigger: Operator observation 2026-05-05 ~21:15Z, after PROP-020 had landed and was working as intended: a manual curmudgeon run still spent meaningful time in the 'mode selection → priority resolution' phase before reaching the actual review. The user noted: 'it was hard to tell when it switched over from mode selection to work.' PROP-020 fixed Priority 3 specifically; this directive examines whether similar precompute-and-hand-off patterns apply elsewhere in the pre-review pipeline.
scope_what_is_in_play: Priority 1 (priority queue): reading monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, identifying which items curmudgeon should service, deduping against tracker.json + recent reviews.
Priority 2 (human notes): reading monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json, filtering for unprocessed entries.
Priority 3 dispatch into change-and-holistic.md (already PROP-020-optimized as of commit 366791d, but the prompt routing and reference-load steps may still carry overhead).
The dispatcher prose itself: monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md is large; if a substantial fraction of input tokens go to dispatch logic vs. review-procedure logic, condensing or splitting may help.
Mode-selection logic: how does curmudgeon decide between Priority 1/2/3 paths? Is there a cheaper precheck that determines the path before loading all the state?
scope_what_is_explicitly_OFF_LIMITS: The actual review procedure (Steps 1-10 in the dispatcher, Lens A/B/C in change-and-holistic.md). Review depth and quality are non-negotiable.
The fingerprint comparison rules (PROP-019 reduced-set + magnitude tiebreak). Already optimal.
PROP-020 drift-audit consumption pattern. Already in place.
Human-notes.json schema or content. Operator owns those.
context.design_intuition: Same pattern as PROP-020: any deterministic work that the LLM currently does in-prompt — file scans, queue resolution, dedup checks, tracker sweeps — is a candidate to be moved to a cheap Node script that runs as part of integrity (or a new precompute helper) and produces an artifact curmudgeon reads. The constraint is: this is the dispatcher, not the work itself. Ship a cheaper dispatcher; don't dilute the review.
context.operator_lean: Operator suspects the biggest wins are: (a) precomputing 'which priority queue items are unsourced and ready for curmudgeon' into a slim file curmudgeon reads in one go; (b) precomputing 'which human notes are pending for curmudgeon' into a similar file; (c) examining whether the curmudgeon.md dispatcher prose itself can be slimmed without losing decision logic. But operator is open to whatever tinker's investigation surfaces.

deliverable_requested.output_file: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json (assign next available PROP number after PROP-020)

structure.instrumentation_pass: Trace what curmudgeon actually does between session-start and entering its review procedure. Look at recent run reports and the actual prompt to estimate: (a) how many file reads happen pre-review, (b) how much input-token weight goes to dispatcher prose vs. review-procedure prose, (c) which decisions are LLM-made vs. could-be-script-made, (d) any redundant reads (e.g., reading the same file twice in different priority checks). Quantify where possible — read counts, byte sizes, prompt-token weights — even rough estimates help.
structure.optimization_options_compared: Lay out at least 4 distinct optimization options. For each: what does it precompute, what artifact does it produce, what does the curmudgeon prompt edit look like, what's the failure mode, what's the estimated savings. Argue tradeoffs. Examples to consider (not exhaustive): (1) Pre-resolved priority queue → write monitor/curmudgeon/queue-pending-for-curmudgeon.json with just the items curmudgeon should service, dedup'd against recent reviews. (2) Pre-filtered human notes → write monitor/curmudgeon/notes-pending.json with just the unprocessed entries. (3) Combine all three priority-check artifacts into a single 'dispatcher-state.json' that curmudgeon reads in one go, replacing three separate file reads + LLM filtering with one read. (4) Slim the curmudgeon.md prompt itself — split rarely-used paths (Mode 4, Mode 5) into reference files loaded only when needed. (5) Add a fast-path early exit when there's clearly nothing to do (e.g., empty queue + no notes + drift-audit unchanged since last review).
structure.recommendation: Pick one or two options that meaningfully cut time-to-review without compromising correctness. Argue. Operator is open to a hybrid (e.g., do the cheap precompute artifacts AND the prompt slim).
structure.implementation_sketch: For the recommended option(s), sketch: (a) any new scripts that need to be created and where they live (monitor/scripts/?), (b) which agent runs the precompute (integrity? a new step in workspace-sync? curmudgeon's own setup?), (c) the prompt diffs to curmudgeon.md and any reference files, (d) a fallback story if the precompute artifact is missing/stale.
structure.regression_protection: How do we know the dispatcher still routes correctly after the optimization? Specify: the existing priority order (queue → human notes → change-detect → holistic → spot-check) must be preserved exactly. The OUTPUT side — review depth, lens application, change_summary discipline — must be unchanged. Pre/post comparison criteria.
structure.interaction_with_PROP-018: PROP-018 (sync manifest) is in flight. The new precompute artifacts proposed here will need to be classified in the manifest when PROP-018 lands. Does this directive's work block on PROP-018, or can it ship independently and get merged into the manifest later? Argue.
structure.estimated_impact: Wall-clock and token reduction estimates for the dispatch phase. Include a 'minimum acceptable improvement' threshold below which the option isn't worth shipping (e.g., <20% dispatch-time reduction = not worth the maintenance cost).
structure.side_effects: What new failure modes does each option introduce? E.g., if pre-resolved queue gets stale, does curmudgeon miss work? Specify the cross-checks that prevent this.

deliverable_requested.do_not_implement: DO NOT modify monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/*, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md, or create any new scripts. Operator reviews PROP and authorizes implementation in a separate step.
open_design_questions_for_tinker_to_addr: Should the precompute artifacts (if any) live in monitor/curmudgeon/ (reader's directory) or monitor/integrity/ (where compute-drift-audit.js's output lives)? Argue consistency with PROP-020's choice.

If a precompute is added, who runs it? Integrity (slow cadence, may be too slow for queue freshness)? Workspace-sync (hourly, already runs frequently)? Curmudgeon's own setup phase (defeats the purpose if it's still in-LLM)? Or a new dedicated cheap script invoked by curmudgeon's setup?
Is there value in instrumentation alongside the optimization — e.g., have curmudgeon emit a 'dispatch_phase_seconds' field in its review JSON so we can measure the impact post-landing? Argue.
Does the holistic-review path (Priority 4) have similar dispatcher-side overhead, or is it cheap? If it's cheap, scope this directive to Priorities 1/2/3 only; if it's expensive, address it too.

Should the optimization preserve the current sequential priority evaluation (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) or could it shift to a parallel-look-then-route pattern (precompute all priorities' state, single LLM read picks the path)? Argue.

non_goals: Do NOT propose changes to the actual review procedure or the lens system.
Do NOT propose changes to the priority-queue schema, human-notes schema, or tracker schema.
Do NOT propose changes to PROP-019 / PROP-020 already-landed work.
Do NOT broaden scope to other agents (decider, analyst). Curmudgeon-only.
Do NOT propose model-tier downgrades for any phase. Stick to Opus.

proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker: PROP-020 is the template — investigate, find deterministic work being done in-LLM, propose moving it to a script + slim the prompt. The constraint is that the review itself stays exactly as deep, careful, and adversarial as it is today. Time saved should come from the path, not the destination.

completion_criteria.file_present: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json exists with all 8 deliverable structure sections.
completion_criteria.instrumentation_concrete: At least rough quantification of where pre-review time/tokens go.
completion_criteria.options_compared: At least 4 options with tradeoffs argued.
completion_criteria.fallback_specified: Each new precompute artifact has a stale/missing handling story.
completion_criteria.open_questions_addressed: All 5 open design questions answered with reasoning.
completion_criteria.mark_directive_completed: Set status:'completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored fields appended per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

cross_references: PROP-020 (template — same precompute-and-hand-off pattern, cold path = integrity)
PROP-019 + commit 71be960 (already-landed change-detect logic; do not modify)
